[[CH10-P1060]] "A hundred out of thousands. A fraction of a fraction." Marnette shook her head. "We need more. Far more. We need the truth to spread until it becomes impossible to contain, until everyone knows someone who knows, until the Masters realise silencing us would mean silencing half the workforce." <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1070]] "We talked about finding allies among the Masters. People who might be willing to help." Petra looked at Lydia, and something close to respect moved across her face. "You're the first to step forward. That takes courage." <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1080]] "Courage or foolishness," Lydia said. "I'm not certain which. But I cannot keep pretending everything is normal. Not after tonight." <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1090]] The tension in the room shifted, not breaking but loosening a little. Trust would take time, if it came at all. But this was something. Lydia was different from them in ways that could never be undone, shaped by privilege and centuries they would never touch. Yet she had chosen to stand with them anyway, to risk her position and perhaps her safety for a truth she had learned hours ago. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1100]] It was not much. One Master, young and relatively powerless, with no seat on the councils that made policy. But it was a beginning. It was proof that the line between Masters and salves might not be as absolute as the system needed them to believe. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1110]] In a revolution built on relationships, even one connection across the divide could change everything. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1120]] *** <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1130]] Lydia proved herself in the weeks that followed. She used her access to Master databases to pull information the network could never reach on its own: details about the Optimisation, names of the Masters who had voted for it, current discussions on population management and resource allocation. She brought back reports of unease spreading among younger Masters who had inherited a system they did not understand and were beginning to ask questions their elders could not answer. <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1140]] "There are others like me," she told Marnette during one of their hidden meetings. "Masters who have noticed the inconsistencies, who have wondered about the lifespan differences, who feel uncomfortable with how salves are treated. They do not know the full truth, but they sense something is wrong." <!-- REV2 -->

[[CH10-P1150]] "Can you reach them? Bring them in the way Chen brought you?" <!-- REV2 -->
